steps.title: Use the area formula
steps.content:
The area of a circle is found from the square of its radius. The problem gives the radius as $32$ millimeters, so use $r=32$ in the circle area formula.
$$A=\pi r^2$$

steps.title: Square the radius
steps.content:
Substitute the radius and compute the square completely.
$$A=\pi(32)^2$$
$$32^2=1{,}024$$
So the area is
$$1{,}024\pi$$
The answer is D.
